prompt_replacements.old: If the question asks for counts per group, by group, or for each specified key, use grouped_aggregate.
prompt_replacements.new: If the question asks for any aggregate measure once per group, including a count, sum, average, minimum, or maximum, use grouped_aggregate.

prompt_replacements.old: Use answer_expression.group_key.domain=SPECIFIED_QUESTION_INPUTS when groups are exactly declared question_inputs; otherwise use SOURCE_RESULT_VALUES.
prompt_replacements.new: Use SPECIFIED_QUESTION_INPUTS only for a closed set of concrete group-member values supplied as question_inputs. Use SOURCE_RESULT_VALUES when group-member values are obtained from source rows.

prompt_replacements.old:
Use grouping_grain when a supplied unit such as day, week, month, or quarter states how source values are divided into result groups.
A grouping_grain is neither the queried time period nor a group member. For 'events per day during March,' day is the grouping_grain and March is the time_value.
prompt_replacements.new:
A grouping_grain is a supplied temporal unit used to derive buckets from a timestamp.
A grouping dimension is an existing source attribute used directly as the group key; it is not a grouping_grain or a question input.

prompt_replacements.old: Use GROUP_KEY when the input defines a requested result group under SPECIFIED_QUESTION_INPUTS.
prompt_replacements.new: GROUP_KEY owns an input whose value will be one result group's key.

prompt_replacements.old: Use GROUP_KEY_DERIVATION when a grouping_grain determines how source values are converted into a SOURCE_RESULT_VALUES group key.
prompt_replacements.new: GROUP_KEY_DERIVATION owns an input that defines how multiple group-key values are derived from source rows.
